---
title: "Core Concepts in Evaluation: A Systematic Approach"
description: "Synthesis note on recurring concepts in Evaluation: A Systematic Approach."
book_id: "evaluation-systematic-approach"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Synthesis"
citation_key: "rossi_lipsey_freeman_2004_evaluation_systematic_approach"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:evaluation-systematic-approach:ch02-chapter-2-tailoring-evaluations:c0002"
  - "chunk:evaluation-systematic-approach:ch03-chapter-3-identifying-issues-and-formulating-questions:c0055"
  - "chunk:evaluation-systematic-approach:ch05-chapter-5-expressing-and-assessing-program-theory:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

This synthesis draws across the generated notes for *Evaluation: A Systematic Approach* [@rossi_lipsey_freeman_2004_evaluation_systematic_approach].

## Central Logic

Evaluation design should be tailored to the question type, program circumstances, stakeholder relationship, and feasible evidence.

## Concepts

- Evaluation question: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Needs assessment: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Program theory assessment: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Process evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Impact assessment: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Efficiency assessment: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.

## Links

- [Tailoring Evaluations](section-01-tailoring-evaluations.qmd)
- [Identifying Issues and Formulating Questions](section-02-formulating-questions.qmd)
- [Expressing and Assessing Program Theory](section-03-program-theory-assessment.qmd)

## References
